title: fixed options not having headless attribute
body:
Bug fix: guard against missing headless attribute on ChromeOptions.

Root cause:
- Chrome.__init__ referenced options.headless directly in multiple places. If a Selenium/Chromium version or a custom ChromeOptions instance does not define a headless attribute, this raises AttributeError (as seen in the PR description).

What this change does:
- Use defensive access (getattr) when reading options.headless.
- When parsing startup args, if a headless flag is detected, set the headless state safely even if the attribute doesn’t exist on the options object.
- Only enable headless configuration if the headless flag is actually present (via getattr), preventing unintended AttributeError.

Impact:
- Fixes the AttributeError in environments where ChromeOptions may not expose a headless attribute.
- Maintains backward compatibility with existing code paths when headless is provided.

Tests to consider:
- Instantiate uc.Chrome with default options (no headless) should not crash.
- Instantiate uc.Chrome with a pre-populated options object that lacks a headless attribute, run through startup and ensure no AttributeError.
- Ensure behavior remains correct when headless=True is passed to Chrome.__init__ and when headless is specified via options.arguments.

step_plan.summary: Bug fix: guard against missing headless attribute on ChromeOptions by using getattr and safely parsing startup args; prevents AttributeError and maintains backward compatibility.

steps.operation: Refactor Chrome.__init__ to robustly handle missing headless attribute on ChromeOptions
steps.reason: To prevent AttributeError when ChromeOptions may not define a headless attribute; preserves compatibility across Selenium/Chromium versions and custom ChromeOptions
steps.side_effects: Tests must cover options without headless; downstream code relying on options.headless should still function; internal behavior changes to use safe getattr; no public API changes
